title: Enable playable songs in track lists

requirements.summary: Add audioSrc values to mock track data and ensure referenced audio files exist under frontend/public/assets/.
acceptanceCriteria: `frontend/src/data/homeContent.ts` trending track items include `audioSrc` for at least 1–3 tracks.
`frontend/src/data/artistContent.ts` track items include `audioSrc` for at least 1–3 tracks.
All referenced `audioSrc` paths resolve to files under `frontend/public/assets/` and load successfully in the browser (no 404).

file_operations.description: Add `audioSrc` (and optionally `fallbackSrc`) to 1–3 items in `trendingTracks`, pointing to audio files under `frontend/public/assets/` (e.g., `/assets/Chahun_main_ya_na.mp3`).
Add `audioSrc` (and optionally `fallbackSrc`) to 1–3 artist `tracks` items (at minimum for the Artist profile popular tracks list), pointing to audio files under `frontend/public/assets/`.
Ensure this audio file exists at this exact path so `audioSrc: '/assets/Chahun_main_ya_na.mp3'` loads successfully (copy/commit the provided mp3 into `frontend/public/assets/`).

requirements.summary: Wire track Play buttons to open the AudioPlayer UI and start playback for the selected track when audioSrc is available.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Play on a trending track starts playback for that track (when `audioSrc` is present).
Clicking Play on an artist’s popular track starts playback for that track (when `audioSrc` is present).
If a track has no `audioSrc`, the UI clearly indicates audio is not available (reusing the existing AudioPlayer fallback behavior is acceptable).

file_operations.description: Create a lightweight playback context/provider that stores the currently selected track metadata for playback and exposes actions like `playTrack(...)` and `closePlayer()` for pages to open the global AudioPlayer.

Wrap routed content with the Playback provider and render a single global `AudioPlayer` UI when a track is selected so Home/Artist Play clicks can open it and show fallback UI when `audioSrc` is missing.
Enhance the existing AudioPlayer to support starting playback immediately when a new selected track is set (e.g., respond to audioSrc/title changes, reset internal state, and optionally auto-play).
Update the Trending Tracks Play overlay button to call the playback context `playTrack(...)` with the clicked track so the global AudioPlayer opens and starts playback when `audioSrc` is available.

Update each Popular Tracks row Play overlay button to call the playback context `playTrack(...)` (including artist name where needed) so the global AudioPlayer opens and starts playback when `audioSrc` is available.

requirements.summary: Ensure only one audio source plays at a time by centralizing playback into a single AudioPlayer instance shared across track lists.
acceptanceCriteria: When a second track is played, the previously playing track is paused/stopped.
There is no overlapping audio from multiple AudioPlayer instances.

file_operations.description: Ensure playback state is singleton (one 'current track' at a time) so new `playTrack(...)` calls replace the existing selection rather than creating multiple players.
Render exactly one AudioPlayer instance at the app layout level (not per card/row) so overlapping instances cannot occur.
On track change, explicitly pause/stop the currently playing audio element before loading/playing the new source to prevent overlapping audio.
